title: The One Where Phoebe Runs
weakest_section: 15:00-16:00 with the naked Ross medical excuse sequence

jokes.text: Rachel and Phoebe struggling over who gets which words for their answering machine message
jokes.setup: Two roommates trying to record an answering machine message together
jokes.punchline: Phoebe complains she only gets boring words like 'it's' and 'and' while Rachel gets the good ones
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven humor that establishes the competitive dynamic between roommates over something trivial

jokes.text: What do I get? I get 'it's,' 'and.' Oh, I'm sorry, I have 'a.' Forget it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's frustration about getting boring words
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic acknowledgment that she also has the thrilling word 'a'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Phoebe's signature passive-aggressive delivery making it funnier

jokes.text: Rachel switches positions then immediately refuses to give up the good words
jokes.setup: Rachel offers to switch after calling Phoebe silly
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals her true selfish nature by keeping the good words
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's competitive streak in a petty but relatable way

jokes.text: Oh, you're no ordinary roommate, are you?
jokes.setup: Phoebe somehow manages to say her parts perfectly despite the word distribution
jokes.punchline: Rachel's mock-dramatic realization delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Decent payoff to the setup with good line delivery potential

jokes.text: There's cute guys there. Let's run towards them!
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting they go running for exercise and bonding
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate pivot to chasing men instead of fitness
jokes.explanation: Classic Rachel moment showing her priorities, though predictable

jokes.text: You took all the words! You've met your match, Rachel Greene!
jokes.setup: The ongoing answering machine word battle
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's triumphant declaration after outsmarting Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good callback that provides satisfying resolution to the opening bit

jokes.text: And I just realized I can sleep with my eyes open.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining fascinating computer technology about downloading consciousness
jokes.punchline: Complete tonal shift to mundane personal revelation
jokes.explanation: Excellent unexpected pivot from high-tech to low-stakes personal observation. Great economy.

jokes.text: Honey, it was a great idea, nailing the boxes to the floor.
jokes.setup: Monica sarcastically praising Chandler
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd impracticality of what he supposedly did
jokes.explanation: Good Monica sarcasm but fairly standard relationship humor

jokes.text: And while I'm doing that, Ross has a great computer story for you.
jokes.setup: Chandler deflecting Monica's irritation about moving boxes
jokes.punchline: Pawning off Ross's boring story on her as revenge/distraction
jokes.explanation: Smart callback showing Chandler's deflection skills and friendship dynamics

jokes.text: And she's gonna live with me!
jokes.setup: Joey introducing his attractive new roommate Janine
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily emphatic clarification of the obvious living arrangement
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey enthusiasm over something obvious, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Joey's overeager responses about not knowing Janine's details
jokes.setup: Normal getting-to-know-you conversation with new roommate
jokes.punchline: Joey's weird emphasis on not knowing basic information about her
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's awkwardness around attractive women but somewhat forced

jokes.text: When can you move in?
jokes.setup: Ross asking what Joey asked Janine during her 'interview'
jokes.punchline: Reveals Joey's entire interview was just asking when she could move in
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - attraction overriding any practical screening. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Now imagine you live at the supermarket.
jokes.setup: Ross remembering how awkward running into ex Donna at supermarket was
jokes.punchline: Chandler's perfect analogy for living with someone you've dated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures the situation with great economy and quotability

jokes.text: You're around me all the time, and you don't flirt. A little sad about that, sweetie?
jokes.setup: Chandler saying it's hard not to flirt around attractive women
jokes.punchline: Monica's pointed observation about his behavior around her
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic humor with Monica's characteristic directness

jokes.text: Phoebe's bizarre running style described as Kermit the Frog meets Six Million Dollar Man
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she doesn't want to run with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: The specific and absurd visual comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy setup with specific, unexpected reference combination that's instantly visualizable

jokes.text: Did you also have his album, 'It's Not Easy Being Green'?
jokes.setup: Ross admitting he also kissed the Six Million Dollar Man poster
jokes.punchline: Confusing the Six Million Dollar Man with Kermit the Frog's signature song
jokes.explanation: Decent callback mixing the references, though the confusion is a bit forced

jokes.text: Pheebs, Monica tripped me. I don't think I can ever run again, ever!
jokes.setup: Rachel needs an excuse to avoid running with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Her dramatically fake injury blame directed at innocent Monica
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's cowardice and willingness to lie, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Ankle. We'll see.
jokes.setup: Monica apologizing for hurting Rachel's 'ankles'
jokes.punchline: Rachel correcting to singular then ominously suggesting it might get worse
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to the fake injury bit with escalating threat

jokes.text: Joey hiding in a box to surprise Janine
jokes.setup: Joey supposedly helping Janine unpack
jokes.punchline: He's been hiding in a box the whole time for a surprise
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey childish behavior, predictable but in character

jokes.text: I don't. No, I want to live with the super-hot Australian dancer!
jokes.setup: Chandler asking sarcastically who wouldn't want to live with Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely honest and inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey honesty cutting through Chandler's sarcasm

jokes.text: How you doing?
jokes.setup: Joey has built-up flirting energy and doesn't know how to release it
jokes.punchline: His signature pickup line delivered inappropriately to Chandler
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey catchphrase used in perfect context showing his misdirected flirting energy

jokes.text: It's just my thongs are too delicate for the dryer.
jokes.setup: Janine apologizing for stuff hanging in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation that sounds suggestive to American ears
jokes.explanation: Good cross-cultural humor with Australian 'thongs' meaning flip-flops

jokes.text: Wow. Couples who live together do start to look alike.
jokes.setup: Seeing Monica and Chandler together
jokes.punchline: Mock observation about couple resemblance (likely both looking frazzled)
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor, likely refers to their shared panic about the apartment

jokes.text: So, Mondler...
jokes.setup: Ross addressing Monica and Chandler as a couple
jokes.punchline: Creating a celebrity-style couple nickname
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau that became cultural shorthand, ahead of its time

jokes.text: Hello? Did you just meet Monica?
jokes.setup: Chandler thinking Monica won't care if he puts her stuff back in the wrong place
jokes.punchline: Ross's incredulous response highlighting Monica's obsessive nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character knowledge joke - Ross knows his sister's control issues better than her boyfriend

jokes.text: Oh, my God. She's going to sit on you.
jokes.setup: Warning Chandler about Monica's potential reaction
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and childish threat that Monica would 'sit on' Chandler
jokes.explanation: Weirdly specific sibling threat that suggests childhood dynamics

jokes.text: Phone's done.
jokes.setup: Monica's increasingly ominous description of her bad day
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact announcement that he's hanging up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan delivery with great economy - two words convey his panic

jokes.text: Monica and Chandler's escalating furniture placement neurosis
jokes.setup: Trying to put furniture back in exact original positions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly complex chain of dependencies where moving one thing affects everything else
jokes.explanation: Good escalating anxiety comedy showing how perfectionism creates impossible problems

jokes.text: But the coffee table doesn't match the table!
jokes.setup: The increasingly complex furniture arrangement discussion
jokes.punchline: Ross's frustrated outburst about furniture matching that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Shows how the perfectionist anxiety is contagious and nonsensical

jokes.text: I'm drawing her to me like a moth to a flame!
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he's not flirting but attracting Janine anyway
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic and clichéd metaphor for his supposed natural magnetism
jokes.explanation: Joey's delusional self-assessment with appropriately cheesy metaphor

jokes.text: You guys repel women all the time!
jokes.setup: Joey asking Ross and Chandler for advice on repelling women
jokes.punchline: His blunt assessment of their natural abilities
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the following sequence where they prove his point

jokes.text: Hey, I've been married three times.
jokes.setup: Ross defending himself against claim he repels women
jokes.punchline: Evidence that actually supports the opposite conclusion (serial failed marriages)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross logic - using his relationship failures as proof of success with women

jokes.text: Irish potato famine discussion at a dance
jokes.setup: Chandler recounting Ross's past romantic failure
jokes.punchline: The absurdly inappropriate topic choice for flirting at a dance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Ross-like choice of depressing historical tragedy as small talk with women

jokes.text: Chandler's 'tsk' sound that Monica doesn't like
jokes.setup: Discussion of things that repel women
jokes.punchline: Chandler's unconscious annoying habit demonstrated physically
jokes.explanation: Good character detail but requires visual element to fully land

jokes.text: That's not specific to girls.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming girls don't like when he talks about science
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation that NOBODY likes Ross's science talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler one-liner with great economy and friend group dynamics

jokes.text: Man, I am so lucky I have Monica. I'm never gonna find love again.
jokes.setup: Listing all their woman-repelling behaviors
jokes.punchline: Chandler's relief contrasted with Ross's despair in same sentence
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing their different relationship situations after the same realization

jokes.text: It's embarrassing. People were looking at us like we were crazy.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to explain why she won't run with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Her shallow concern about strangers' opinions over friendship
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's vanity but predictable character flaw

jokes.text: People you don't know and will never see again. Yes, but still they are people... with eyes.
jokes.setup: Phoebe pointing out the strangers don't matter
jokes.punchline: Rachel's ridiculous emphasis on the strangers having 'eyes'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's vanity into absurd territory

jokes.text: I run like I did when I was a kid, because that's the only way it's fun... like when you were running towards the swings or running away from Satan?
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her carefree running philosophy
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of running away from Satan as childhood experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe weirdness - treating Satan encounters as normal childhood memories

jokes.text: The neighbor's dog.
jokes.setup: Rachel presumably asking for clarification about Satan
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact correction/clarification
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan follow-up - whether clarifying or just naming the dog Satan

jokes.text: Who cares? I repel women.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about furniture placement
jokes.punchline: Ross's defeated acceptance of his dating fate making furniture irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how the relationship revelation affects other life areas

jokes.text: Why is Ross naked?
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming Ross is naked to keep Monica out
jokes.punchline: Monica's reasonable question about this bizarre situation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalating naked lie sequence reasonably well

jokes.text: I had to show Chandler something.
jokes.setup: Monica asking why Ross is supposedly naked
jokes.punchline: Ross's innocent explanation that sounds suggestive
jokes.explanation: Fairly predictable innuendo, relies on cheap double meaning

jokes.text: Naked? I guess I have... a 'guy' problem.
jokes.setup: Continuing to justify the nakedness
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward euphemism for a medical issue
jokes.explanation: Awkward escalation of the lie with typical Ross discomfort

jokes.text: There's still some of Chandler's medicine in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming to have the same problem Chandler had
jokes.punchline: Monica's helpful suggestion revealing Chandler had a similar 'guy problem'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that implicates Chandler in an embarrassing medical situation

jokes.text: What'd you have?
jokes.setup: The mysterious medical condition conversation
jokes.punchline: Ross's persistent curiosity about Chandler's fake medical problem
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Ross's curiosity, simple but effective

jokes.text: Don't you mean 'for whom'?
jokes.setup: Joey offering beer 'for you'
jokes.punchline: Janine's grammatical correction
jokes.explanation: Callback to the grammar correction as woman-repellent, but Joey actually likes it

jokes.text: Pizza? It's not like I never had that before.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting they share pizza
jokes.punchline: Janine's oddly defensive response about pizza experience
jokes.explanation: Shows cultural/social awkwardness but not particularly strong

jokes.text: You're hot. I'm lovable. Clearly, there's a vibe going on between us.
jokes.setup: Joey deciding to address the attraction directly
jokes.punchline: His blunt, self-aggrandizing assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey confidence and directness, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Imagine living in a supermarket and you'll understand.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why they can't date
jokes.punchline: Using Chandler's earlier analogy almost verbatim
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey actually listened to advice and can apply metaphors

jokes.text: I'm not interested in you like that.
jokes.setup: Joey's elaborate explanation of why they shouldn't date
jokes.punchline: Janine's simple rejection revealing his whole speech was unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Joey's ego and assumptions - excellent structure

jokes.text: It's because I was trying to repel you. Right? Believe me, you'd feel a lot different if I turned it on.
jokes.setup: Janine's rejection
jokes.punchline: Joey's delusional explanation that he was deliberately repelling her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey inability to accept rejection, with callback to repelling strategy

jokes.text: How you doing?
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he could turn on the charm if he wanted
jokes.punchline: His signature pickup line delivered to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Second use of the catchphrase in perfect context, showing Joey's one-trick approach to flirting

jokes.text: I'm so sorry. You're right. This feels great! And you don't care if people stare. It's only a second. Then you're gone!
jokes.setup: Rachel learning to run like Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Her complete conversion to Phoebe's philosophy after experiencing it
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment with Rachel embracing Phoebe's free-spirited approach

jokes.text: Watch out for the horse.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Phoebe running freely through the park
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of a horse as an obstacle in Central Park
jokes.explanation: Great unexpected detail that adds to the chaotic energy of their running

jokes.text: I don't see anything different... other than the fact that the room got brighter when you came into it.
jokes.setup: Monica noticing everything in the apartment is different
jokes.punchline: Chandler's cheesy romantic deflection attempt
jokes.explanation: Predictable romantic cheese from panicking Chandler, but in character

jokes.text: Did Ross sit anywhere while he was naked?
jokes.setup: Monica accepting Chandler's thoughtful gesture
jokes.punchline: Her immediate pivot to hygiene concerns about Ross's nakedness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica priorities - romantic moment interrupted by cleanliness concerns

jokes.text: Janine, do you know what we're doing right now?
jokes.setup: Ross repeating his computer consciousness speech to Janine
jokes.punchline: His creepy attempt to make it sound romantic or meaningful
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ross using his boring speech as a pickup line

jokes.text: You and I, we're interfacing.
jokes.setup: Ross's computer-human interface explanation
jokes.punchline: Using technical jargon as a romantic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross attempt at being smooth using computer terminology - appropriately awkward

jokes.text: I gotta go.
jokes.setup: Ross's awkward computer interface pickup line
jokes.punchline: Janine's immediate need to escape the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Ross's awkwardness with great economy - two words say everything

tmdb_overview: Phoebe and Rachel start running together. Joey finds an attractive roommate. Chandler reorganizes the apartment.
